title: Goa 2-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Goa 2-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Goa, India: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Dudhsagar Waterfalls jeep safari and Spice plantation tour with traditional lunch. Prices from $1409 per person (estimate).
highlights: Spice plantation tour with traditional lunch
Sunset cruise on the Mandovi River
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Goa, India — Tropical climate with a hot, humid monsoon season (Jun-Sep) bringing heavy rainfall, and a warm, dry winter season (Nov-Feb) that is peak tourist season. November to February for cool, dry, sunny weather ideal for beaches and sightseeing. This itinerary blends Dudhsagar Waterfalls jeep safari, Spice plantation tour with traditional lunch, Baga Beach with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Dudhsagar Waterfalls jeep safari, Spice plantation tour with traditional lunch, Baga Beach
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Goa
itinerary.description: Land at Manohar International Airport / Dabolim Airport (GOI) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Dudhsagar Waterfalls jeep safari — Off-road trip to one of India's tallest waterfalls. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Goa Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to Spice plantation tour with traditional lunch — Guided walk through a working spice farm. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to GOI for your onward flight.
activities: Spice plantation tour with traditional lunch

travelTips: Rent a scooter for easy beach-hopping, but wear a helmet and carry an international license
Visit North Goa for nightlife and South Goa for quieter, upscale beaches
Avoid the monsoon season (Jun-Sep) for swimming due to rough seas
Bargain at flea markets but stay respectful
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Lightweight breathable clothing for year-round heat
Modest cover-up clothing for temple and church visits
Insect repellent for evenings near paddy fields and forests
thingsToDo: Spice plantation tour with traditional lunch
Casino cruise on the Mandovi River
thingsToAvoid: Swimming during monsoon season when rip currents are dangerous and lifeguard coverage drops
Riding a scooter without a helmet or license — police checks are common
Public nudity or topless sunbathing, which is illegal
Overpaying unlicensed taxi drivers — negotiate or use ride apps
topRestaurants: Vinayak Family Restaurant — Goan seafood thali ($)
Curlie's Beach Shack (Anjuna) — Goan and international ($$)
luxuryExperiences: Beachfront villa stay at a five-star South Goa resort
Private yacht cruise on the Mandovi River
Private candlelit dinner on the beach
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset cruise on the Mandovi River
Private beach dinner in South Goa
Secluded stay in a Palolem beach villa
budgetExperiences: Free beach days at Baga or Calangute

festivalCalendar.name: Feast of St. Francis Xavier (Old Goa)

localEtiquette: Dress modestly away from beaches, especially at temples and churches (cover shoulders and knees).
Remove shoes before entering temples and some traditional homes.
Nudity and topless sunbathing are illegal and culturally offensive.
Bargain politely at markets rather than aggressively.
Respect Goa's blended Hindu-Catholic-Portuguese heritage in dress and conduct at religious sites.
localTransportGuide: Renting a scooter or motorbike is the most popular way to get around Goa's beach towns, though an international driving permit and helmet are strongly advised. Uber and Ola operate in the main towns, and hired taxis are common for day trips to further-flung sights like Dudhsagar Falls.
bestTimeToVisit: November to February for cool, dry, sunny weather ideal for beaches and sightseeing.
weather: Tropical climate with a hot, humid monsoon season (Jun-Sep) bringing heavy rainfall, and a warm, dry winter season (Nov-Feb) that is peak tourist season.
visaInformation: India requires an e-Visa for most foreign nationalities (excluding SAARC countries under separate arrangements), applied for online before arrival and typically valid for stays of 30-90 days depending on the category; the passport must have at least six months' validity.
averageFlightTime: ~9.8h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Goa?
faqs.answer: India requires an e-Visa for most foreign nationalities (excluding SAARC countries under separate arrangements), applied for online before arrival and typically valid for stays of 30-90 days depending on the category; the passport must have at least six months' validity.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Goa?
faqs.answer: November to February for cool, dry, sunny weather ideal for beaches and sightseeing. Tropical climate with a hot, humid monsoon season (Jun-Sep) bringing heavy rainfall, and a warm, dry winter season (Nov-Feb) that is peak tourist season.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Goa safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Goa is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Goa?
faqs.answer: The local currency is INR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Goa is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunset cruise on the Mandovi River experience.

reviews.author: Lukas Johnson
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Goa is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Naval Aviation Museum. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Larsen
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Big Foot Museum & crafts village alone was worth the trip, but combined with Fort Aguada lighthouse this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Sarah Rossi
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Goa is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Dudhsagar Waterfalls jeep safari. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Ava Santos
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Spice plantation tour with traditional lunch alone was worth the trip, but combined with Chapora Fort ruins this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Ava Tanaka
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Butterfly Beach boat trip alone was worth the trip, but combined with Arambol Beach this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Noah Fernandez
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Our trip to Goa was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Spice plantation tour with traditional lunch was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Chen Smith
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Fort Aguada lighthouse in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.
